subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Pillars of Creation
description: One-minute skill: The Pillars of Creation.

hook.text: The Pillars of Creation are towering columns of gas and dust inside the Eagle Nebula—one of astronomy's most famous images.

buildup.text: Hubble captured them in 1995. The tallest pillar is about 5 light-years tall—larger than the distance to the nearest star.

discovery.text: Young stars form inside the pillar tips. Radiation from nearby massive stars slowly erodes the columns from outside.

twist.text: Infrared images show the pillars are transparent at longer wavelengths. Baby stars glow through the dust like hidden jewels.

climax.text: JWST re-imaged the Pillars in 2022 with stunning infrared detail, revealing newborn stars invisible to Hubble.

punchline.text: Columns of gas five light-years tall. Stars are being born inside them right now.

quiz.question: What is happening inside the Pillars of Creation?
options: New stars are forming inside the dense gas while radiation from nearby stars erodes the pillars
Ancient stars are dying and collapsing into black holes
Dark matter is accumulating and will eventually form a new galaxy
